sections.title: Executive Overview
sections.body: This report presents a corporate finance FP&A analysis of Tesla, Inc. (TSLA), covering the period 2021–2025. In 2025, the company reported revenue of $94.83B (-2.9% YoY) and net income of $3.79B, with a net margin of 4.0%. The analysis integrates P&L trends, cash flow quality, working capital efficiency, budget variance, forward forecasts, and sensitivity testing to support management-style financial planning and decision-making.

sections.title: P&L Performance
sections.body: Gross margin expanded from 17.9% to 18.0% (+0.2pp), while operating margin compressed from 7.9% to 5.1% (-2.8pp) in 2025. The year-over-year net income bridge (2024 → 2025) shows a total change of $-3.34B, comprising revenue volume ($-511.4M), margin/mix ($155.4M), operating expense ($-2.56B), below-the-line items ($-425.0M).

sections.title: Cash Flow & Liquidity
sections.body: In 2025, operating cash flow reached $14.75B with free cash flow of $6.22B (FCF margin: 6.6%). The OCF-to-net-income ratio of 3.89x indicates strong earnings quality — cash generation comfortably exceeds reported profits. CapEx intensity was 9.0% of revenue, reflecting continued capital investment in the business.

sections.title: Working Capital & Capital Structure
sections.body: Return on equity was 4.6% and ROIC was 5.4% in 2025. DuPont decomposition shows net margin of 4.0%, asset turnover of 0.69x, and equity multiplier of 1.68x. Working capital efficiency: cash conversion cycle of 13 days (DSO 18d, DIO 58d, DPO 63d). Capital structure remains conservative with a net cash position (net debt/EBITDA of -0.15x) and interest coverage of 14.3x, indicating ample liquidity and debt service capacity.

sections.title: Budget vs Actual
sections.body: Compared to the forward budget built on stated growth assumptions, 2025 actuals underperformed across all key metrics. Revenue missed budget by -13.3% and net income by -57.0%, classified as unfavorable variance. Operating income variance was -58.8% — the largest relative gap — suggesting margin compression and cost overruns relative to plan were the primary drivers of the budget shortfall.

sections.title: Forecast Outlook
sections.body: Base-case revenue forecast projects $119.84B by 2028, using exponential smoothing on historical trends. Scenario analysis ranges from $81.30B (bear) to $185.21B (bull) in 2028, a 86.7% spread relative to the base forecast for strategic planning.

sections.title: Sensitivity & Key Risks
sections.body: Sensitivity analysis (±10% driver changes) identifies Cost Of Revenue as the highest-impact variable on net income, with a net income swing of $12.16B. Total Revenue ranks second with a $2.56B net income swing. FP&A teams should prioritize these drivers in scenario planning and management reporting.

sections.title: Conclusion
sections.body: Tesla, Inc. faces growth deceleration and margin pressure in the current cycle, yet maintains positive free cash flow and a strong balance sheet. Key FP&A priorities include: (1) monitoring gross margin trends and cost discipline, (2) sustaining recent working capital improvements, (3) recalibrating budgets to reflect actual performance gaps, and (4) stress-testing forecasts against revenue and COGS sensitivities. This analysis framework is replicable across any public company using standardized Yahoo Finance data.

summary.fullText:
Executive Overview
This report presents a corporate finance FP&A analysis of Tesla, Inc. (TSLA), covering the period 2021–2025. In 2025, the company reported revenue of $94.83B (-2.9% YoY) and net income of $3.79B, with a net margin of 4.0%. The analysis integrates P&L trends, cash flow quality, working capital efficiency, budget variance, forward forecasts, and sensitivity testing to support management-style financial planning and decision-making.

P&L Performance
Gross margin expanded from 17.9% to 18.0% (+0.2pp), while operating margin compressed from 7.9% to 5.1% (-2.8pp) in 2025. The year-over-year net income bridge (2024 → 2025) shows a total change of $-3.34B, comprising revenue volume ($-511.4M), margin/mix ($155.4M), operating expense ($-2.56B), below-the-line items ($-425.0M).

Cash Flow & Liquidity
In 2025, operating cash flow reached $14.75B with free cash flow of $6.22B (FCF margin: 6.6%). The OCF-to-net-income ratio of 3.89x indicates strong earnings quality — cash generation comfortably exceeds reported profits. CapEx intensity was 9.0% of revenue, reflecting continued capital investment in the business.

Working Capital & Capital Structure
Return on equity was 4.6% and ROIC was 5.4% in 2025. DuPont decomposition shows net margin of 4.0%, asset turnover of 0.69x, and equity multiplier of 1.68x. Working capital efficiency: cash conversion cycle of 13 days (DSO 18d, DIO 58d, DPO 63d). Capital structure remains conservative with a net cash position (net debt/EBITDA of -0.15x) and interest coverage of 14.3x, indicating ample liquidity and debt service capacity.

Budget vs Actual
Compared to the forward budget built on stated growth assumptions, 2025 actuals underperformed across all key metrics. Revenue missed budget by -13.3% and net income by -57.0%, classified as unfavorable variance. Operating income variance was -58.8% — the largest relative gap — suggesting margin compression and cost overruns relative to plan were the primary drivers of the budget shortfall.

Forecast Outlook
Base-case revenue forecast projects $119.84B by 2028, using exponential smoothing on historical trends. Scenario analysis ranges from $81.30B (bear) to $185.21B (bull) in 2028, a 86.7% spread relative to the base forecast for strategic planning.

Sensitivity & Key Risks
Sensitivity analysis (±10% driver changes) identifies Cost Of Revenue as the highest-impact variable on net income, with a net income swing of $12.16B. Total Revenue ranks second with a $2.56B net income swing. FP&A teams should prioritize these drivers in scenario planning and management reporting.

Conclusion
Tesla, Inc. faces growth deceleration and margin pressure in the current cycle, yet maintains positive free cash flow and a strong balance sheet. Key FP&A priorities include: (1) monitoring gross margin trends and cost discipline, (2) sustaining recent working capital improvements, (3) recalibrating budgets to reflect actual performance gaps, and (4) stress-testing forecasts against revenue and COGS sensitivities. This analysis framework is replicable across any public company using standardized Yahoo Finance data.
